name: AXS 1h Retest Hold Timeout v1
edge_mechanism: a structural break is retested and held, but exposure is cut by time if expansion does not appear quickly, avoiding slow bleed continuation trades
thesis: Cycle 74 explicitly wants retest-and-hold continuation that de-risks by time if expansion fails to appear fast enough. AXS 1h is the best-fit structurally different lane because it is active enough to test whether faster timeout logic can rescue continuation from slow decay. The edge is the held retest plus strict time discipline, not passive hope.
invalidation_condition: If AXS 1h retest holds are either too chaotic or too sparse, or if time-based de-risking still cannot prevent bleed, the thesis fails.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price breaks a local boundary, retests that level, holds it, and should expand quickly if the continuation is real.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Real continuation often shows itself quickly after an accepted retest. If expansion does not appear fast, the trade is often wrong or stale. Timeout de-risking should reduce that failure mode.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: AXS 1h is the best fit for this management experiment because it is more reactive than ETH/BTC and makes fast feedback from timeout logic easier to observe.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: AXS may simply be too noisy, turning the retest-hold grammar into false positives even with timeout discipline.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries exist but timeout is too aggressive, extend the de-risk clock slightly before loosening the retest filter.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Plenty of retest attempts, many early scratches, and a minority of fast-expanding continuations that justify the structure.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: AXS 1h clears density and timeout de-risking meaningfully reduces slow-bleed continuation losses.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-20 trades, or a profile where retest entries still bleed despite the timeout rule.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: low on 1h with simple retest-hold grammar

validation_basket.why_included: Best-fit structurally different lane for testing timeout-protected retest continuation.
validation_basket.continue_if: AXS 1h shows enough retest-hold events and timeout de-risking reduces bleed.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: AXS 1h remains too chaotic for the structure to matter.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: High participation with quick feedback on whether the continuation is real.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same timeout-protected retest grammar survives on the primary lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH 1h preserves enough retest-hold continuation with cleaner structure.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: ETH 1h becomes too flat or too sparse for the mechanism.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Cleaner, slightly lower-count retest continuations than AXS.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same timeout management works on a cleaner similar asset.
validation_basket.continue_if: BTC 1h shows coherent retest-hold continuation with fewer slow bleeds.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: BTC 1h confirms the idea is too dependent on AXS volatility.

long: Time-based de-risk: cut 50% after 4 bars if no 0.9 ATR expansion
short: Time-based de-risk: cut 50% after 4 bars if no 0.9 ATR expansion
